items.name: System description
items.description:
MIB: SNMPv2-MIB.
The textual description of the entity. This value should include the full name and version identification number of the system's hardware type, software operating-system, and the networking software.

triggers.expression: change(/.Cisco Generic by SNMP/system.hw.serialnumber)=1 and length(last(/.Cisco Generic by SNMP/system.hw.serialnumber))>0
triggers.name: Cisco ASR9K: Device has been replaced
triggers.event_name: Cisco ASR9K: Device has been replaced (new serial number received)
triggers.description: The serial number of a device has changed. Acknowledge to close the problem manually.

triggers.expression: change(/.Cisco Generic by SNMP/system.sw.os)=1 and length(last(/.Cisco Generic by SNMP/system.sw.os))>0
triggers.name: Cisco ASR9K: Operating system description has changed
triggers.description: The description of the operating system has changed. Possible reasons that system has been updated or replaced. Acknowledge to close the problem manually.

discovery_rules.name: CPU discovery
discovery_rules.description:
You must use CISCO-PROCESS-MIB and its object `cpmCPUTotal5minRev` from the table called `cpmCPUTotalTable`, and indexed with `cpmCPUTotalPhysicalIndex`.
The table `cpmCPUTotalTable` allows CISCO-PROCESS-MIB to keep the CPU statistics for different physical entities in the router, such as different CPU chips, a group of CPUs, or CPUs in different modules/cards.
In the case of a single CPU, the `cpmCPUTotalTable` has only one entry.

item_prototypes.name: #{#SNMPINDEX}: CPU utilization
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: CISCO-PROCESS-MIB
The object name: `cpmCPUTotal5minRev`

The MIB object `cpmCPUTotal5minRev` provides a more accurate view of the performance of the router over time than the MIB objects `cpmCPUTotal1minRev` and `cpmCPUTotal5secRev`. These MIB objects are not accurate because they look at the CPU with an interval of one minute and five seconds, respectively. These MIBs enable to monitor the trends and plan the capacity of your network. The recommended baseline rising threshold for the `cpmCPUTotal5minRev` is 90 percent. Depending on the platform, some routers that run at 90 percent, for example, 2500 series can exhibit performance degradation versus a high-end router, for example, the 7500 series, which can operate fine.

trigger_prototypes.name: #{#SNMPINDEX}: High CPU utilization
trigger_prototypes.description: The CPU utilization is too high. The system might be slow to respond.

discovery_rules.name: Entity serial numbers discovery
discovery_rules.description: The discovery of serial numbers of the entities from ENTITY-MIB.

item_prototypes.name: {#ENT_NAME}: Hardware serial number
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: ENTITY-MIB.
The object name: `entPhysicalSerialNum`.
The vendor-specific serial number string for the physical entity. The preferred value is the serial number string actually printed on the component itself (if present).

trigger_prototypes.expression: change(/.Cisco Generic by SNMP/system.hw.serialnumber[{#SNMPINDEX}])=1 and length(last(/.Cisco Generic by SNMP/system.hw.serialnumber[{#SNMPINDEX}]))>0
trigger_prototypes.name: {#ENT_NAME}: Device has been replaced
trigger_prototypes.event_name: {#ENT_NAME}: Device has been replaced (new serial number received)
trigger_prototypes.description: The device serial number has changed. Acknowledge to close the problem manually.

discovery_rules.name: Fan status discovery
discovery_rules.description: The discovery of metrics for the fan's status from ENTITY-MIB and CISCO-ENTITY-FRU-CONTROL-MIB.

item_prototypes.name: {#SNMPVALUE}: Fan operational status

trigger_prototypes.name: {#SNMPVALUE}: Fan is down
trigger_prototypes.description: The fan unit requires immediate attention.

discovery_rules.name: Memory discovery
discovery_rules.description:
The discovery of `ciscoMemoryPoolTable` - the table that contains monitoring entries of the memory pool.
For more details see "How to Get Free and Largest Block of Contiguous Memory Using SNMP":
http://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/support/docs/ip/simple-network-management-protocol-snmp/15216-contiguous-memory.html

item_prototypes.name: {#SNMPVALUE}: Free memory
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: CISCO-ENHANCED-MEMPOOL-MIB.
The object name: `cempMemPoolFree`.
It indicates the number of bytes from the memory pool that are currently unused on the physical entity.

item_prototypes.name: {#SNMPVALUE}: Used memory
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: CISCO-ENHANCED-MEMPOOL-MIB.
The object name: `cempMemPoolUsed`.
It indicates the number of bytes from the memory pool that are currently in use by applications on the physical entity.

trigger_prototypes.name: {#SNMPVALUE}: High memory utilization
trigger_prototypes.description: The system is running out of free memory.

discovery_rules.name: PSU discovery
discovery_rules.description: The discovery of power supplies from ENTITY-MIB and CISCO-ENTITY-FRU-CONTROL-MIB.

item_prototypes.name: {#SNMPVALUE}: Power supply status

trigger_prototypes.name: {#SNMPVALUE}: PSU is in failed state
trigger_prototypes.description: The FRU is in a failed state.

trigger_prototypes.name: {#SNMPVALUE}: PSU is off or out of optimal state

item_prototypes.name: {#SENSOR_NAME}: Temperature Sensor Status
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: CISCO-ENTITY-SENSOR-MIB.
The object name: entSensorStatus.
This variable indicates the present operational status of the sensor.
Possible values:
-  ok (1): means the agent can read the sensor value;
-  unavailable (2): means that the agent presently can not report the sensor value;
-  nonoperational (3) means that the agent believes the sensor is broken. The sensor could have a hard failure (e.g., disconnected wire), or a soft failure (e.g., out-of-range, jittery, or wildly fluctuating readings).

trigger_prototypes.expression: last(/.Cisco Generic by SNMP/system.sfp.light[{#SNMPINDEX}]) > last(/.Cisco Generic by SNMP/entSensorThresholdRxTxValueHigh[{#SNMPINDEX}])
trigger_prototypes.name: Cisco ASR9K: {#SENSOR_IF}: Light Level is above the critical threshold

trigger_prototypes.expression: last(/.Cisco Generic by SNMP/system.sfp.light[{#SNMPINDEX}]) > last(/.Cisco Generic by SNMP/entSensorThresholdRxTxValueHighWarn[{#SNMPINDEX}])
trigger_prototypes.name: Cisco ASR9K: {#SENSOR_IF}: Light Level is above the warning threshold

dependencies.name: Cisco ASR9K: {#SENSOR_IF}: Light Level is above the critical threshold
dependencies.expression: last(/.Cisco Generic by SNMP/system.sfp.light[{#SNMPINDEX}]) > last(/.Cisco Generic by SNMP/entSensorThresholdRxTxValueHigh[{#SNMPINDEX}])

trigger_prototypes.expression: last(/.Cisco Generic by SNMP/system.sfp.light[{#SNMPINDEX}]) < last(/.Cisco Generic by SNMP/entSensorThresholdRxTxValueLow[{#SNMPINDEX}])
trigger_prototypes.name: Cisco ASR9K: {#SENSOR_IF}: Light Level is below the critical threshold

trigger_prototypes.expression: last(/.Cisco Generic by SNMP/system.sfp.light[{#SNMPINDEX}]) < last(/.Cisco Generic by SNMP/entSensorThresholdRxTxValueLowWarn[{#SNMPINDEX}])
trigger_prototypes.name: Cisco ASR9K: {#SENSOR_IF}: Light Level is below the warning threshold

dependencies.name: Cisco ASR9K: {#SENSOR_IF}: Light Level is below the critical threshold
dependencies.expression: last(/.Cisco Generic by SNMP/system.sfp.light[{#SNMPINDEX}]) < last(/.Cisco Generic by SNMP/entSensorThresholdRxTxValueLow[{#SNMPINDEX}])

discovery_rules.name: Temperature sensors discovery
discovery_rules.description: The discovery of temperature sensors from CISCO-ENTITY-SENSOR-MIB and ENTITY-MIB. The sensors that have celsius (8) `entSensorType` are discovered. The scale of gathered values is taken from the `entSensorScale` and applied in item preprocessing.

item_prototypes.name: {#SNMPVALUE}: Temperature sensor status
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: CISCO-ENTITY-SENSOR-MIB.
The object name: entSensorStatus.
This variable indicates the present operational status of the sensor.
Possible values:
-  ok (1): means the agent can read the sensor value;
-  unavailable (2): means that the agent presently can not report the sensor value;
-  nonoperational (3) means that the agent believes the sensor is broken. The sensor could have a hard failure (e.g., disconnected wire), or a soft failure (e.g., out-of-range, jittery, or wildly fluctuating readings).

trigger_prototypes.name: {#SNMPVALUE}: Temperature sensor is not available
trigger_prototypes.description: It means that the agent presently can not report the sensor value.

trigger_prototypes.name: {#SNMPVALUE}: Temperature sensor is not operational
trigger_prototypes.description: It means that the agent considers that the sensor is broken. The sensor could have a hard failure (e.g., disconnected wire), or a soft failure (e.g., out-of-range, jittery, or wildly fluctuating readings).

item_prototypes.name: {#SNMPVALUE}: Temperature
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: CISCO-ENTITY-SENSOR-MIB.
The object name: entSensorValue.
This variable reports the most recent measurement seen by the sensor.

trigger_prototypes.name: {#SNMPVALUE}: Temperature is above critical threshold
trigger_prototypes.description: This trigger uses the values of the temperature sensor.

trigger_prototypes.name: {#SNMPVALUE}: Temperature is above warning threshold
trigger_prototypes.description: This trigger uses the values of the temperature sensor.

macros.description: It leaves only the matching fan names as indicated in the filter string.
It leaves only the matching power supply names as indicated in the filter string.
The filter excludes chassis (3) class from Serial discovery. The chassis (3) are polled with a regular item.
The filter retrieves only existing serial number strings.
The PSU states list for average trigger priority.
